manifest.description: E1-S3 golden baseline: a root container holding a variable-box that holds at least one widget from every family — string (text-input, textarea), number (number-field, slider, stepper, the latter two carrying min/max/step range config), boolean (toggle, checkbox), enum (select, radio-group, segmented, each with an option set), and array (multiselect + checkbox-group with option sets, tag-input freeform). The box's widget children are held directly (not block-wrapped). Captures the CURRENT pre-migration resolved-tree shape of every built-in variable-widget type so the dogfood migration can be proven byte-identical.
manifest.title: All Widget Families

Placeholder text shown in the empty control — editable at runtime.
How the box arranges its variable widgets — editable at runtime. `stacked` lays them out one per row; `inline` flows them along a single row. Semantic and renderer-agnostic: NO CSS units or keywords.

The container's relative-weight grid (E3-S2 surface). Editable at runtime: the column track weights, the row track weights, and the inter-track gap — all unitless, relative numbers. Column and row weights are positive-number arrays the resolver normalizes to fractions summing to 1 per axis; gap is a non-negative number in the same relative space. NO CSS units or keywords.

The container grid's column track weights (E2-S1 nested surface): a positive-number array the resolver normalizes to fractions summing to 1, editable on its own without touching the row tracks or the gap. NO CSS units or keywords.

The container grid's inter-track gap (E2-S1 nested surface): a non-negative, unitless number in the same relative space as the track weights, editable on its own without touching the column/row tracks. NO CSS units or keywords.

The container grid's row track weights (E2-S1 nested surface): a positive-number array the resolver normalizes to fractions summing to 1, editable on its own without touching the column tracks or the gap. NO CSS units or keywords.
